name: Caer Darrow
region: Island fortress in Darrowmere Lake, Lordaeron.

sensoryProfile.sight: Island walls, slick black stone at the waterline, an upper window left open a hand's width, and old fortress stone carrying institutional traffic instead of noble domestic life.
sensoryProfile.sound: Mist-thinned quiet outside, ward hum through the walls, and then the building's own interior signals passing downward once the breach is made.
sensoryProfile.smell: Cold wet masonry outside, then preserving salts, dust, and old blood from the service levels within.
sensoryProfile.touch: Seams between lake-slick stones, wet mortar beneath the hands, and a keep whose outer wall already feels like part of the academy below it.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: A fortress that can still be infiltrated, but only through the neglected edge rather than the face it shows visitors.

establishedFacts.fact: Caer Darrow remains a defensible island keep repurposed into live research space rather than abandoned ruin.
The strongest plague-support signature sits beneath the visible fortress levels.
External relay traffic confirms Caer Darrow is active enough that a ferry watch can mark entrants and notice who does not come back out.
Its deepest defenses answer intrusion from the top down, handing movement through the keep in layered ward responses that can be felt in the sanctum below.
The east face of Caer Darrow carries less traffic than the public landing and can be breached through an upper service window when watched routes are too costly.
The keep's outer shell, upper service rooms, and lower Scholomance core operate as one layered defensive environment.

eventsHere.event: Jaina arrives on the island and confirms the deeper source lies under the keep rather than in the estate surface behind her.
Farstrider relays report that Jaina crossed to Caer Darrow before dusk and had not re-emerged by nightfall.
The keep itself begins relaying Sylvanas's descent toward the sanctum through one answered threshold after another.
Sylvanas breaches Caer Darrow through the east-face window while Cyndia holds the boat and exit line below.
The island releases Sylvanas and Jaina only after the sanctum fight, with Cyndia taking them back across before the south road claims them.
